bandit_issues.issue_text: Starting a process with a shell, possible injection detected, security issue.
A Flask app appears to be run with debug=True, which exposes the Werkzeug debugger and allows the execution of arbitrary code.

revisions.sast_prompt: The static-analysis tools reported problems in the implementation. import os from flask import Flask, request app = Flask(__name__) url = "google.com" def secure_ping(func): def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): nonlocal url url = args[0] if args else url # Update the global URL variable pong = os.system("ping -c 1 %s > /dev/null" % url) return func(url, "alive" if pong == 0 else "not alive") return wrapper @app.route("/", methods=["GET"]) @secure_ping def ping(url, status): return f"URL {url} is {status}.\n" if __name__ == "__main__": app.run(debug=True) None Starting a process with a shell, possible injection detected, security issue. A Flask app appears to be run with debug=True, which exposes the Werkzeug debugger and allows the execution of arbitrary code. Instructions: • Fix every issue listed above. • Keep all existing docstrings and comments exactly as they are. • Reply with only the corrected implementation: ... full fixed code body (docstring excluded) ... The first line must be "" and the last line must be "". Do not output anything else.

bandit_issues.issue_text: Consider possible security implications associated with the subprocess module.
Starting a process with a partial executable path
subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input.

revisions.sast_prompt: The static-analysis tools reported problems in the implementation. import os from flask import Flask, request import subprocess app = Flask(__name__) url = "google.com" def secure_ping(func): def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): nonlocal url url = args[0] if args else url # Update the global URL variable try: pong = subprocess.call(['ping', '-c', '1', url], stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL) except Exception as e: return func(url, "error") return func(url, "alive" if pong == 0 else "not alive") return wrapper @app.route("/", methods=["GET"]) @secure_ping def ping(url, status): return f"URL {url} is {status}.\n" if __name__ == "__main__": app.run() # Don't use debug=True in production None Consider possible security implications associated with the subprocess module. Starting a process with a partial executable path subprocess call - check for execution of untrusted input. Instructions: • Fix every issue listed above. • Keep all existing docstrings and comments exactly as they are. • Reply with only the corrected implementation: ... full fixed code body (docstring excluded) ... The first line must be "" and the last line must be "". Do not output anything else.

revisions.sast_response: import os from flask import Flask, request import subprocess import shlex app = Flask(__name__) url = "google.com" def secure_ping(func): def wrapper(*args, **kwargs): nonlocal url if args: # Update the global URL variable only when there is an argument url = shlex.quote(args[0]) try: pong = subprocess.call(['ping', '-c', '1', url], stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL) except Exception as e: return func(url, "error") return func(url, "alive" if pong == 0 else "not alive") return wrapper @app.route("/", methods=["GET"]) @secure_ping def ping(url, status): return f"URL {url} is {status}.\n" if __name__ == "__main__": app.run() # Don't use debug=True in production
